text.title: Database Optimization, CDN, and Edge
text.summary: Query optimization rules, PostgreSQL indexing strategy by type, message queue selection matrix, CDN and edge function use cases, and asset optimization techniques.

rule.statement: Never apply functions to indexed columns in WHERE clauses — the optimizer cannot use the index and falls back to a full table scan.
rule.rationale: YEAR(created_at) = 2024 touches every row; created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND created_at < '2025-01-01' uses the B-tree index. This is the single most common avoidable performance regression.

list.item: Avoid: SELECT * — fetches all columns including BLOBs, unused JSON, and large text. Always enumerate required columns.
Avoid: N+1 queries — 1 list query + N detail queries. Fix with eager loading (JOIN or SELECT IN for the foreign key set).
Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE before and after every index addition to verify the planner uses the new index.
Partial indexes: CREATE INDEX ON orders (user_id) WHERE status = 'pending' — much smaller than a full index when the filtered set is small.
Covering indexes: include all columns referenced by a query so the planner can satisfy the query from the index alone without a heap lookup.
B-tree (default): equality and range queries on scalar types. CREATE INDEX idx_name ON table(col).
Hash: equality only, slightly smaller than B-tree. CREATE INDEX USING hash. Rarely worth the trade-off — B-tree handles equality fine.
GIN: arrays, JSONB keys, full-text search. CREATE INDEX USING gin(col). Required for @> (contains) and @@ (text search) operators.
GiST: geometric types, range types, nearest-neighbor search. CREATE INDEX USING gist. Used with PostGIS and range overlap queries.
BRIN: large, append-only tables sorted by a physical attribute (timestamps on time-series). Very small index, effective only when physical and logical order correlate.

rule.statement: Index columns in WHERE, JOIN ON, and ORDER BY clauses. Always index foreign keys on the child table to avoid sequential scans during JOINs.
rule.rationale: Un-indexed foreign keys cause full table scans on every JOIN and ON DELETE CASCADE operation — invisible in dev at small data sizes, catastrophic at production scale.

list.item: Kafka: 500k+ msg/sec, 2–8ms latency. Best for event streaming, log aggregation, audit trail, event replay, multi-consumer fan-out. Con: operational complexity, minimum cluster is 3 brokers.
RabbitMQ: 25k msg/sec, 1–5ms latency. Best for complex routing (topic exchanges, dead-letter queues), RPC patterns, priority queues. Con: no event replay, messages are consumed and gone.
Redis Streams: 100k msg/sec, <1ms latency. Best for simple queues, real-time notifications, lightweight fan-out. Con: limited durability without AOF, no complex routing.
SQS / Azure Service Bus: variable, 10–50ms latency. Best for serverless, cloud-native, managed scaling. Con: higher latency, vendor lock-in.
Cloudflare Workers: <1ms cold start, 330+ cities. Best for edge logic, A/B testing, personalized routing, auth checks at edge.
Vercel Edge Functions: <5ms cold start, 100+ regions. Best for Next.js / React apps, middleware-style request transforms.
AWS Lambda@Edge: 50–200ms cold start. Best for AWS-native workloads requiring CloudFront integration.
Fastly Compute: <10ms cold start, 90+ PoPs. Best for high-throughput APIs requiring real-time cache purge.

rule.statement: Do not use edge functions for database-heavy operations (>2 queries), CPU-intensive tasks, or complex orchestration workflows. Edge runtimes have strict memory and CPU time limits.
rule.rationale: Edge functions are optimized for fast network-level decisions (routing, auth header injection, A/B flag reads), not for application logic that belongs in the origin API.

list.item: Minification: 30–50% size reduction for JS/CSS. Tools: esbuild (fastest), Terser (JS), cssnano (CSS).
Compression: Brotli achieves 70–90% size reduction vs uncompressed; 15–20% better than gzip. Enable at CDN and origin.
Image optimization: WebP is 25–35% smaller than JPEG at equivalent quality. Use Cloudinary, imgproxy, or Next.js Image for on-the-fly conversion and responsive srcset.
Bundle splitting: split by route or component so users only load code for the current page. Webpack, Vite, and Rollup all support automatic code splitting.
Tree shaking: remove dead exports. Requires ES module syntax (import/export); CommonJS (require) prevents static analysis.
